question_title: Will GWW's market close price on 2026-03-12 be higher than its market close price on 2026-03-03?
summary: GWW closed at $1140.77 on March 3, 2026. The question asks whether the close on March 12 (7 trading days later) will be higher. Key evidence: (1) Conditional base rate when GWW is in 5%+ drawdown from 52w high shows 54.1% positive over 7 days from 1201 historical events; (2) Recent 20-day bootstrap MC yields 52.3%; (3) Zero-mean bootstrap gives 51.3%; (4) Broader market weakness (S&P 500 falling) provides a headwind. The stock is consolidating after a sharp post-earnings drop from ~$1202 to ~$1116. Net assessment is very close to a coin flip with a slight positive tilt from the drawdown conditional, partially offset by current market weakness.

factors.description: Unconditional base rate: stocks go up slightly more than 50% over any 7-day period
GWW conditional base rate: when in 5%+ drawdown from 52w high, 54.1% positive in next 7 days (1201 historical events)
Recent 20-day bootstrap MC (post-earnings crash consolidation period): 52.3% chance of being higher
Broader market weakness: S&P 500 down ~2.7% from recent peak, today down another ~1.3%, headwind for individual stocks
GWW down ~6.4% from 52w high of $1218.63, consolidating in $1105-1152 range for ~3 weeks after sharp post-earnings drop
60-day sample includes anomalous +5.4% earnings jump on Feb 3, inflating full-sample bootstrap; recent period more representative

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.52 (52%)**

This is essentially a coin flip with a very slight upward tilt. Key reasoning:

- **7 trading days horizon** from March 3 close ($1,140.77) to March 12 close
- **Conditional base rate**: When GWW is in a 5%+ drawdown from its 52-week high (currently -6.4% from $1,218.63), it's been positive over the next 7 days 54.1% of the time across 1,201 historical episodes
- **Recent momentum is slightly negative**: The post-earnings crash consolidation period (last 20 days) shows a mean daily return of -0.06%, with the bootstrap MC yielding ~52.3%
- **Market headwind**: The S&P 500 is under pressure, down to ~6,793 today, which drags on individual stocks
- **Net**: The mild drawdown reversion signal is partially offset by current market weakness, landing near 52%
